- Top point: (3, 21)
- Then a gap, then (9, 27)

- Then rows 21-39 form the right half of the diamond

- Row 39: (39, 39) - bottom-right corner
Wait, this doesn't look like a simple diamond. Let me reconsider. The red dots form:
- (3, 21) - top
- (9, 27) - a point below
- (12, 12) - left
- Then from row 21 to 39, there's a diamond-like shape with two columns per row
Actually looking at the pattern:
- Rows 21-30: left column goes from 39 to 30, right column from 39 to... 
- Row 21: cols 39
- Row 22: cols 38, 40
- Row 23: cols 37, 41
...
- Row 30: col 30
- Row 31: cols 31, 47
...
- Row 39: col 39
This looks like a diamond rotated 45 degrees, but the top part is incomplete. The full diamond would have:
- Top: (21, 39) - but there's also (3, 21) and (9, 27) and (12, 12)
Hmm, these top points (3,21), (9,27), (12,12) seem disconnected from the main diamond body. Let me reconsider.
Actually wait - (3, 21), (9, 27), and (12, 12) could be part of a larger diamond that's rotated. The main body from rows 21-39 is clearly a diamond shape centered around (30, 39) with the point at (30, 30) at the bottom.
Actually, let me reconsider the whole structure. The white dot is at [30, 39]. Looking at the red positions:
- Row 21: col 39
- Row 30: col 30
- Row 39: col 39
So the diamond has corners at approximately (21, 39), (30, 30), (39, 39), and (30, 48)... wait that doesn't work either.
Let me think about this differently. The white dot at [30, 39] is the center of the red diamond. The diamond outline extends:
- Up: to row 21, col 39
- Down: to row 39, col 39
- Left: to col 30 (at row 30)
- Right: to col 48 (at row 30)
So the diamond is centered at [30, 39] with radius 9 in both directions. The outline follows |r-30| + |c-39| = 9.
Let me verify:
- (21, 39): |21-30| + |39-39| = 9 ✓
- (30, 30): |30-30| + |30-39| = 9 ✓
- (39, 39): |39-30| + |39-39| = 9 ✓
- (30, 48): |30-30| + |48-39| = 9 ✓
- (22, 38): |22-30| + |38-39| = 8+1 = 9 ✓
- (22, 40): |22-30| + |40-39| = 8+1 = 9 ✓
Yes! The red diamond is centered at [30, 39] with radius 9.
But what about the other red points: (3, 21), (9, 27), (12, 12)?
- (3, 21): |3-30| + |21-39| = 27+18 = 45 ≠ 9
- (9, 27): |9-30| + |27-39| = 21+12 = 33 ≠ 9
- (12, 12): |12-30| + |12-39| = 18+27 = 45 ≠ 9
These are NOT part of the diamond. They must be something else. Let me check if they're part of the orange X or something else.
Wait, I had 38 red 1px dots. Let me count the diamond:
- Row 21: 1 (col 39)
- Rows 22-29: 2 each = 16
- Row 30: 1 (col 30)
- Rows 31-38: 2 each = 16
- Row 39: 1 (col 39)
Total: 1 + 16 + 1 + 16 + 1 = 35
But I counted 38 red dots. So there are 38 - 35 = 3 extra red dots: (3, 21), (9, 27), (12, 12).
These 3 extra red dots are likely targets or markers. Let me check if they're inside gray boxes.
Looking back at the gray boxes:
- Box at ~[2, 20], center color=r, center at [3, 21] ← matches!
- Box at ~[8, 26], center color=r, center at [9, 27] ← matches!
- Box at ~[11, 11], center color=r, center at [12, 12] ← matches!
So these 3 red dots are the centers of gray boxes. They're targets.
Now I understand the game better:
- There are 3 shapes: orange X, red diamond, blue cross
- SPACE switches the active shape (moves white dot to a different shape's center)
- Arrow keys move the active shape
- Gray boxes contain colored targets (r, b, O)
- The goal might be to collect targets by moving shapes over them
But wait, the white dot at [30, 39] is the center of the red diamond. When I press SPACE, it switched from the orange X center to the red diamond center. Let me try SPACE again to see if it switches to the blue cross.
Actually, I need to reconsider. In level 1, SPACE switched between two crosses. Here, there are 3 shapes. Let me test SPACE again.
But first, let me understand the movement. When I pressed RIGHT on the orange X, it moved right by 3 pixels. The movement step size seems to be 3.
Now, the goal: I think the goal is to collect all the colored targets (the centers of gray boxes). The targets have colors r (red), b (blue), and O (orange). When a shape's center passes over a target, it gets collected.
Let me check: the gray boxes have centers at:
- [3, 21] - red
- [9, 27] - red
- [12, 12] - red
- [36, 27] - blue
- [39, 9] - orange
- [42, 24] - orange
- [48, 15] - blue
- [48, 33] - blue
- [57, 9] - orange
- [60, 27] - blue
That's 10 targets total. The shapes are:
1. Orange X - center at [18, 30] (after RIGHT from [18, 27])
2. Red diamond - center at [30, 39]
3. Blue cross - center at some position
Let me figure out the blue cross center. The blue cross has 53 pixels. Looking at the boundary: [[29, 48], [41, 48], [42, 49], [42, 61], ...]. This looks like a cross shape.
Let me try SPACE again to switch to the blue cross and see where the white dot goes.
